arguments.task: Open and read the strongest official source pages for Mermaid CLI. Start at https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli and inspect the repository README, package/usage information, and any linked official documentation needed to verify: what Mermaid CLI is, whether using it requires any authentication, whether access is self-serve free or otherwise gated, what API surface exists (CLI/package vs REST/GraphQL/none), and whether there is any official MCP server noted in first-party docs. Also inspect the repository license and release/install details if helpful. Return concise extracted findings with the exact page URLs actually read.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect first-party GitHub repository pages and supporting official docs for verified product facts.

raw_result.description: Command-line and Node.js package for rendering Mermaid diagram definitions into image and document outputs.
raw_result.auth_notes: No first-party authentication method was found in the repository materials reviewed; the official GitHub repository presents Mermaid CLI as an installable local CLI/Node package rather than a hosted authenticated API.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official repository is publicly accessible and MIT-licensed, indicating self-serve access without signup or sales gating for the software itself.

api_surface.breadth: No hosted REST or GraphQL API was evidenced in the reviewed first-party pages; the surfaced interface is a local CLI binary (`mmdc`) plus a programmatic Node.js package entrypoint (`./src/index.js`).
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or MCP documentation was evidenced in the reviewed first-party repository pages.

raw_result.blocker: No hosted web API surface was evidenced; toolkit construction would need to wrap the local CLI or Node package instead of integrating with a documented SaaS API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No API rate limits were evidenced because no hosted API surface was found in the reviewed first-party materials.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public GitHub repository; MIT license; package metadata indicates an executable `mmdc` CLI and module entrypoint, supporting local automation use cases.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field `mcp_exists` is based on absence of evidence within the reviewed repository pages rather than an explicit first-party statement that no official MCP exists.
